title: How to Deal with Spiritual Confusion
meta_description: Discover why spiritual confusion isn't failure. You're not broken. You're shedding old beliefs before new ones form. This is the messy middle of awakening.
og_title: How to Deal with Spiritual Confusion
og_description: Discover why spiritual confusion isn't failure. You're not broken. You're shedding old beliefs before new ones form. This is the messy middle of awakening.
body:
You're Not Losing Your Mind. You're Losing The Map.

Let me guess. You used to know what you believed. You had a approach. Maybe it was religion, maybe it was science, maybe it was just the quiet certainty that things made sense. And then something cracked. A weird coincidence that felt too pointed to ignore. A dream that came true. A feeling in your chest that told you someone was lying before they opened their mouth. And now you're standing in the middle of your own life, looking around, thinking, "What the hell is actually real?"

That's spiritual confusion. And I want to say something to you that nobody else is going to say: You're not doing it wrong. You're not broken. You haven't wandered off the path. You're just in the part of the journey where the old map stops working and the new one hasn't fully formed yet. And that's terrifying. Right?! It's like being in a fog where every step feels like a guess.

I've been there. I spent years trying to piece together a belief system that could hold all the strange, contradictory things I was experiencing. I read everything. I meditated until my legs went numb. I chased teachers and traditions like they were breadcrumbs out of the woods. And you know what I found? More confusion. Because the problem wasn't that I didn't have enough information. The problem was that I was trying to use my mind to solve something my mind had never been designed to handle.

"Spiritual confusion isn't a sign that you've lost the truth. It's a sign that your old version of truth has outgrown its container. You're not confused because you're wrong. You're confused because you're expanding."

Why Spiritual Confusion Hits So Hard (And Why It's Actually A Good Sign)

First, let's normalize this. Spiritual confusion is not a glitch in the system. It's a feature of genuine awakening. When you start to wake up to the wider reality - the one where energy is real, where intuition speaks, where you can feel someone's mood from across the room - your entire operating system has to reboot. And rebooting feels like chaos.

Think about it. You've spent your whole life building a model of reality based on what you could see, touch, and prove. That model worked. It got you through school, through relationships, through your career. But now you're having experiences that don't fit in that model. You're picking up on things you shouldn't know. You're feeling emotions that aren't yours. You're sensing presences in empty rooms. And your rational mind is screaming, "This doesn't compute!"

That scream is spiritual confusion. It's the sound of your brain trying to shove a square peg into a round hole. And the more you try to force it, the more confused you get. So what do you do? You stop forcing. You let the old model dissolve. And you trust that a new one will form in its place.

Does that land? I know it sounds scary. But here's the thing: every single person who has walked a genuine spiritual path has gone through this. The mystics, the saints, the psychics, the shamans - they all hit a wall where nothing made sense anymore. They called it the "dark night of the soul." I call it the bright wound. Because it hurts like hell, but it's also the place where the most light gets in.

Stop Trying To Figure It All Out Right Now

This is the biggest mistake I see. Someone gets spiritually confused, and their first instinct is to find the answer. They go online, they buy courses, they join groups, they ask everyone for their opinion. They're trying to think their way out of confusion. And it doesn't work. Because confusion at this level isn't a thinking problem. It's a being problem.

Your mind wants certainty. It wants a clear box with a label on it. But the spiritual path doesn't give you a box. It gives you a horizon. And you can't fit a horizon into a box. So stop trying.

Here's what I want you to do instead: Get comfortable with not knowing. I know that sounds impossible, but it's actually a skill you can build. Start small. When you feel that familiar panic of "I don't know what's true anymore," just say to yourself, "Okay. I don't know. And that's allowed." Don't try to fix it. Don't try to answer it. Just let it be there.

I recommend picking up a copy of The Untethered Soul by Michael A. Singer. It's one of the best books I've found for learning how to sit in the not-knowing without losing your grip. See on Amazon (paid link) It'll change how you relate to the confusion.

Ground Yourself In Your Body, Not Your Head

Here's a hard truth: spiritual confusion lives in your head. It's a mental loop. And the only way out of a mental loop is to drop into your body. Your body doesn't care about your beliefs. Your body doesn't need to know if reincarnation is real or if angels exist. Your body just needs to breathe, to feel, to be present.

So when the confusion gets loud, stop thinking. Put your hand on your chest. Feel your heartbeat. Feel your feet on the floor. Take three slow breaths. That's it. You don't need to solve anything in that moment. You just need to come back to the one thing that's always true: you're here, you're alive, and you're okay right now.

I know it sounds simple. But simple works. The most advanced spiritual teachers I know all come back to the same thing: presence. Not knowledge. Not certainty. Presence. Because presence is where you can hold confusion without being destroyed by it.

If you want a deeper dive into this, check out The Power of Now by Eckhart Tolle. It's a classic for a reason. See on Amazon (paid link)

Stop Collecting Beliefs Like Stamps

One of the sneakiest traps of spiritual confusion is the urge to collect beliefs. You hear about past lives, and you think, "That makes sense!" Then you hear about channeling, and you think, "Maybe that too!" Then you hear about astrology, and crystals, and karma, and shadow work, and before you know it, you've got a whole shelf of spiritual ideas that don't actually fit together. And you're more confused than ever.

Here's the thing: You don't need to believe everything. In fact, you don't need to believe anything. You just need to be open. Open to experience. Open to learning. Open to changing your mind. But you don't have to sign up for every spiritual system that comes along.

Think of it like this: you're not building a belief system. You're building a relationship with the unknown. And relationships don't work when you're trying to collect all the facts first. They work when you show up, pay attention, and let the truth reveal itself over time.

So give yourself permission to be a skeptic. Give yourself permission to say, "I don't know if that's true, and I'm okay with waiting to find out." That's not a lack of faith. That's wisdom.

Trust Your Own Experience Over Anyone Else's Map

This is the big one. And it's hard. Because when you're confused, you look for someone who seems certain. You look for a guru, a teacher, a book, a system that promises to make it all clear. And there are plenty of people out there who will sell you certainty. But here's the problem: their certainty is based on their experience, not yours.

Your spiritual path is yours. It's shaped by your history, your wiring, your wounds, your gifts. What works for someone else might not work for you. And what's true for someone else might not be true for you. That doesn't mean they're wrong and you're right. It just means that spiritual truth is not one-size-fits-all.

So start trusting your own experience. If a practice makes you feel more alive, do it. If a teacher makes you feel small, leave. If a belief resonates in your gut, hold it lightly. If it doesn't, let it go. Your intuition is your best guide here. And yes, your intuition might feel rusty or unreliable right now. That's okay. It gets stronger the more you use it.

One book that helped me a ton with this is The Psychic Pathway by Sonia Choquette. It's a practical, no-nonsense guide to trusting your own inner knowing. See on Amazon (paid link)

Create A Simple Daily Practice (And Stick To It)

Spiritual confusion thrives on chaos. When your life is scattered, your mind is scattered, and your spirit is scattered, the confusion gets louder. The antidote is not more information. It's structure. Simple, repeatable structure.

I'm not talking about a three-hour meditation routine. I'm talking about five minutes a day where you stop, breathe, and check in with yourself. That's it. You can do it in the morning before you check your phone. You can do it at night before you sleep. You can do it in the middle of the day when you feel the confusion creeping in.

Here's a simple practice that works:

  • Sit somewhere quiet. Close your eyes.
  • Take three slow breaths. In through your nose, out through your mouth.
  • Put your hand on your heart. Ask yourself: "What am I feeling right now?"
  • Don't judge the answer. Just notice it.
  • Then ask: "What do I need right now?"
  • Listen. Don't force an answer. Just listen.

That's it. Do that every day for a month, and I promise you, the confusion will start to settle. Not because you found the answers. But because you built a relationship with yourself that's stronger than the chaos.

For more on building a grounded spiritual practice, I highly recommend Real Magic by Dean Radin. It's a science-based look at how psychic abilities work, and it'll give you a approach that doesn't require blind faith. See on Amazon (paid link)

Let Go Of The Need To Be "Spiritual Enough"

Here's something nobody talks about: spiritual confusion often comes with a side of spiritual shame. You feel like you should have this figured out by now. You feel like everyone else is more advanced, more certain, more connected. You feel like you're failing at something that's supposed to be natural.

Stop that. Right now. There is no such thing as "spiritual enough." There's no finish line. There's no hierarchy. You don't get a gold star for having the clearest channel or the most deep insights. You're just a human being, walking a path, doing your best. And that's enough.

The people who seem the most spiritually certain are often the most fragile. Real depth comes with real humility. Real wisdom comes with real questions. If you're confused, you're in good company. Every genuine mystic I've ever met has said the same thing: the more they know, the more they realize they don't know.

Find Your People (But Not Too Many Of Them)

You can't do this alone. Spiritual confusion gets worse when you isolate. You need at least one or two people who get it. People who won't look at you like you're crazy when you talk about feeling energies or having strange dreams. People who can sit with you in the not-knowing without trying to fix you.

But here's the catch: don't join too many groups. Don't flood your feed with spiritual content. Don't get into debates about whose path is right. A few good friends, a trusted teacher, a single community - that's enough. Too many voices will just amplify the confusion.

Look for people who are grounded. People who have been walking this path for a while and still have their feet on the ground. People who laugh easily and don't take themselves too seriously. Those are the ones worth listening to.

Remember: Confusion Is A Door, Not A Wall

I want to leave you with this. Spiritual confusion feels like a wall. It feels like you've hit a dead end. But it's not a wall. It's a door. And the door only opens when you stop trying to force it.

Think of confusion as a kind of spiritual compost. All those old beliefs, all those broken maps, all those questions without answers - they're not garbage. They're fertilizer. They break down and become the soil for something new to grow. But you have to let them break down. You have to let the old fall apart before the new can take root.

So be patient with yourself. Be gentle. You're not lost. You're just in the in-between. And the in-between is sacred. It's the place where transformation happens. It's the place where you shed the skin that no longer fits. It's the place where you learn to trust something deeper than your mind.

You're going to be okay. More than okay. You're going to come out of this with a clarity that's not borrowed from anyone else. A clarity that's yours. And it's going to be worth every single moment of confusion.

If you're ready to go deeper and find some clarity on where you are in your own awakening, take our free assessment. It'll help you see the bigger picture of what you're going through. Click here to take the assessment or try the quick quiz.
